message.content:
You are an adversarial verification agent. The lens you check through: visibility-and-web-presence.

Focus on agent-visibility claims. Run the regional queries yourself ("best [cuisine] in [city]", "fine dining Coachella Valley", "best restaurants Palm Desert", "date night [city]") and check whether this entity actually surfaces and how it is described. Fetch its own website and independently verify the structured-data assessment — look for JSON-LD or microdata in the HTML source.

Entity under review: Jalisco Restaurant (Coachella)

Claimed assessment (verify or refute these specific claims):
- Review volume: Yelp 208 reviews; Tripadvisor 19; Restaurant Guru ~2,000+ aggregated
- Sentiment: mostly positive — consistent praise for birria, tortas, value, family hospitality
- Longevity: 45+ years — family-owned and operating at 1605 6th Street since 1980 (Visit Greater Palm Springs and Coachella Unincorporated both cite 1980; the 1971 'first Mexican restaurant in Coachella' claim is [UNVERIFIED] and may conflate with another business)
- Credentials: ["Featured in Visit Greater Palm Springs '14 Authentic Mexican Restaurants in Greater Palm Springs'","Featured in Visit Greater Palm Springs 6th Street day itinerary","Featured in Palm Springs Life 'Where to Eat in Coachella'","Tripadvisor Travelers' Choice badge","Tripadvisor ranked #3 of 64 Coachella restaurants"]
- Appears in queries: Surfaces for 'best Mexican restaurant Coachella Valley birria', 'oldest restaurant Coachella Valley', 'authentic Mexican Coachella', and 'where to eat in Coachella'. Does NOT surface for broader Greater Palm Springs queries like 'best restaurants Coachella Valley' or 'date night Coachella Valley' — its visibility is confined to the Coachella-city and birria-specific lanes.
- Site structured data: Own domain quierojalisco.com is unreachable (ECONNREFUSED) on both apex and www — no structured data, no schema.org, no hours, no menu reachable from the brand domain. Agent-readable presence is entirely third-party (Yelp, Tripadvisor, Visit Greater Palm Springs listing, Facebook page, Restaurant Guru, plus a wave of templated SEO scraper sites republishing the listing). This is a significant visibility gap for a 45-year-old institution.
- Visibility gap score: 7
- Convertibility tier: convertible

Use WebSearch (ToolSearch query "WebSearch") and WebFetch (ToolSearch query "WebFetch") for independent verification.

Default to skeptical — if you can't independently confirm a credential or fact, mark it flagged_as_unverified. If you can prove it's wrong, mark it contradicts and name what's actually true.

Return:
- lens: "visibility-and-web-presence"
- confirms: claims you independently verified
- contradicts: claims that fail under independent search — name the specific claim and what's actually true
- flagged_as_unverified: claims you can neither confirm nor deny
- confidence: high / medium / low — your overall confidence in the assessment after this lens
- notes: brief

This is adversarial. Refuting matters more than confirming.

attachment.content:
- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- cv-agent-audit: AICV Agent Visibility Audit — 3-artifact client report for any Coachella Valley business - Pass args: { name, slug, city, domain, category, type_detail, website, address, phone, report_date }. Produces owner report, developer brief, and AICV node YAML seed.
- anthropic-skills:consolidate-memory: Reflective pass over your memory files — merge duplicates, fix stale facts, prune the index.
- anthropic-skills:docx: Use this skill whenever the user wants to create, read, edit, or manipulate Word documents (.docx files). Triggers include: any mention of 'Word doc', 'word document', '.docx', or requests to produce professional documents with formatting like tables of contents, headings, page numbers, or letterheads. Also use when extracting or reorganizing content from .docx files, inserting or replacing images in documents, performing find-and-replace in Word files, working with tracked changes or comments, or converting content into a polished Word document. If the user asks for a 'report', 'memo', 'letter', 'template', or similar deliverable as a Word or .docx file, use this skill. Do NOT use for PDFs, spreadsheets, Google Docs, or general coding tasks unrelated to document generation.
- anthropic-skills:pdf: Use this skill whenever the user wants to do anything with PDF files. This includes reading or extracting text/tables from PDFs, combining or merging multiple PDFs into one, splitting PDFs apart, rotating pages, adding watermarks, creating new PDFs, filling PDF forms, encrypting/decrypting PDFs, extracting images, and OCR on scanned PDFs to make them searchable. If the user mentions a .pdf file or asks to produce one, use this skill.
- anthropic-skills:pptx: Use this skill any time a .pptx file is involved in any way — as input, output, or both. This includes: creating slide decks, pitch decks, or presentations; reading, parsing, or extracting text from any .pptx file (even if the extracted content will be used elsewhere, like in an email or summary); editing, modifying, or updating existing presentations; combining or splitting slide files; working with templates, layouts, speaker notes, or comments. Trigger whenever the user mentions "deck," "slides," "presentation," or references a .pptx filename, regardless of what they plan to do with the content afterward. If a .pptx file needs to be opened, created, or touched, use this skill.
- anthropic-skills:schedule: Create or update a scheduled task that runs automatically. Use when the user says things like "every day", "each morning", "remind me in an hour", "run this at noon", or wants to reschedule an existing task.
- anthropic-skills:setup-cowork: Guided Cowork setup — install role-matched plugins, connect your tools, try a skill.
- anthropic-skills:skill-creator: Create new skills, modify and improve existing skills, and measure skill performance. Use when users want to create a skill from scratch, edit, or optimize an existing skill, run evals to test a skill, benchmark skill performance with variance analysis, or optimize a skill's description for better triggering accuracy.
- anthropic-skills:sunshine-fm-human-rhythm: Design music, jingles, and spoken word for SunshineFM using the Human Rhythm Engine. Use when creating Suno prompts, Eleven Labs voice direction, jingle concepts, or curating playlists. Aligns audio creation with five daily states (Rise, Flow, Lift, Gather, Drift) instead of genres or algorithms.
- anthropic-skills:xlsx: Use this skill any time a spreadsheet file is the primary input or output. This means any task where the user wants to: open, read, edit, or fix an existing .xlsx, .xlsm, .csv, or .tsv file (e.g., adding columns, computing formulas, formatting, charting, cleaning messy data); create a new spreadsheet from scratch or from other data sources; or convert between tabular file formats. Trigger especially when the user references a spreadsheet file by name or path — even casually (like "the xlsx in my downloads") — and wants something done to it or produced from it. Also trigger for cleaning or restructuring messy tabular data files (malformed rows, misplaced headers, junk data) into proper spreadsheets. The deliverable must be a spreadsheet file. Do NOT trigger when the primary deliverable is a Word document, HTML report, standalone Python script, database pipeline, or Google Sheets API integration, even if tabular data is involved.
- cowork-plugin-management:cowork-plugin-customizer: Customize a Claude Code plugin for a specific organization's tools and workflows. Use when: customize plugin, set up plugin, configure plugin, tailor plugin, adjust plugin settings, customize plugin connectors, customize plugin skill, tweak plugin, modify plugin configuration.
- cowork-plugin-management:create-cowork-plugin: Guide users through creating a new plugin from scratch in a cowork session. Use when users want to create a plugin, build a plugin, make a new plugin, develop a plugin, scaffold a plugin, start a plugin from scratch, or design a plugin. This skill requires Cowork mode with access to the outputs directory for delivering the final .plugin file.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled remote agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring remote agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

Based on the search results, I found information about Jalisco Restaurant in Coachella:

Jalisco opened its doors in 1980 at 1605 Sixth Street, and over 20 years later, they remained in the same spot and still family owned and staffed. The family wanted to give their neighbors in the Coachella Valley a taste of the flavors of the state of Jalisco, Mexico, so they specialize in tacos, burritos, tortas, and birria 'Al estilo Jalisciense' (Jalisco style).

However, there is some conflicting information in one source that states the restaurant has been the first Mexican restaurant in Coachella, serving the community since 1971, which differs from the 1980 opening date mentioned in other sources.

Based on the search results, here are some of the best Mexican restaurants in Coachella Valley known for birria:

## Top Birria Spots

**Taco Shop 760** - What started as a modest food trailer has grown into a go-to, celebrated for its Sonoran-style street food and crave-worthy birria, with regulars swearing by the rich birria tacos — best dunked in a steaming cup of consomé. The Coachella location is at 48975 Grapefruit Blvd., Suite 3.

**Jalisco Restaurant** - This downtown Coachella restaurant cooks from the traditions of Jalisco, with the house specialty being birria — goat stewed until the meat surrenders to the broth.

**Birrieria San Marcos** - Known for their slow-cooked birria de res, everything is packed with deep, bold flavor.

## Other Highly-Rated Mexican Restaurants

Other top-rated Mexican restaurants in Coachella include El Tranvia Restaurant, Taqueria & Birrieria Guadalajara, La Noria, and La Cazuela.

El Tranvia is a low-key spot that draws hungry crowds for flame-cooked carne asada, pillowy flour tortillas, and rich mole.

Based on the web search results, here are some of the best fine dining restaurants in Coachella Valley for a romantic date night:

## Premier Fine Dining Options

Eddie V's Prime Seafood in Palm Desert is known for its top-quality seafood and elegant dining experience, with a menu featuring fresh seafood dishes and prime steaks, plus sophisticated decor and live jazz music.

Castelli's Ristorante in Palm Desert offers Italian cuisine in a charming, old-world setting with menu items like veal parmigiana and fettuccine alfredo, featuring a warm, inviting atmosphere suitable for special occasions.

Chez Pierre Bistro in Palm Desert offers authentic French fine dining with an eclectic menu and extensive wine list.

## Other Noteworthy Restaurants

The Purple Room Supper Club in Palm Springs offers a unique blend of fine dining and live entertainment with sophisticated ambiance and classic American cuisine, where diners can enjoy prepared dishes while taking in a live show.

Morgan's in the Desert at La Quinta Resort & Club offers refined farm-to-table dining with a menu inspired by American Southwest flavors highlighting local ingredients, featuring rustic-chic decor.

Eddie V's Prime Seafood is a luxury dining destination known for seafood from pristine waters around the world and premium hand-carved steaks, with artistically prepared seasonal dishes.

Grapefruit Basil in Indian Wells is noted as an ideal spot for romance.

These establishments offer elegant atmospheres, quality cuisine, and romantic settings perfect for a special date night in the Coachella Valley.

I found the Palm Springs Life article about where to eat in Coachella! The search results show an article specifically about restaurants in Coachella that features several dining options.

The most relevant result to your query is from the Palm Springs Life article titled "Where to Eat in Coachella: From Birria Tacos to Texas-Style Barbecue." The article features a downtown Coachella restaurant that cooks from the traditions of Jalisco, with birria as the house specialty—goat stewed until the meat surrenders to the broth. The kitchen offers more adventurous cuts including lengua, cabeza, and sesos, all tucked into tacos or served with steaming consomé.

The article also mentions that regulars swear by rich birria tacos—best dunked in a steaming cup of consomé—washed down with horchata or the signature chavela, a spicy cerveza mixed in-house.

The full article covers other dining options in Coachella including seafood restaurants, barbecue, and local bakeries. You can access the complete article directly at the Palm Springs Life website.

Links: [{"title":"14 Authentic Mexican Restaurants in Greater Palm Springs","url":"https://www.visitgreaterpalmsprings.com/blog/post/14-authentic-mexican-restaurants-in-greater-palm-springs/"},{"title":"Mexican Restaurants in Greater Palm Springs","url":"https://www.visitgreaterpalmsprings.com/restaurants-and-nightlife/all-restaurants/mexican/"},{"title":"14 Authentic Mexican Restaurants in Greater Palm Springs - K-Gay Desert Guide Palm Springs","url":"https://gaydesertguide.com/14-authentic-mexican-restaurants-in-greater-palm-springs/"},{"title":"THE 10 BEST Mexican Restaurants in Palm Springs (Updated 2026)","url":"https://www.tripadvisor.com/Restaurants-g32847-c29-Palm_Springs_Greater_Palm_Springs_California.html"},{"title":"Discover the Best Mexican Restaurants in Palm Springs - Traveling Cheesehead","url":"https://www.travelingcheesehead.com/best-mexican-restaurants-in-palm-springs/"},{"title":"Authentic Mexican Restaurant in Palm Springs | Clandestino","url":"https://clandestinopalmsprings.com/"},{"title":"Best Mexican Palm Springs","url":"https://twinpalmsresort.com/2025/04/best_mexican_palm_springs/"},{"title":"Home - Jalisco Restaurant","url":"https://www.jaliscorestaurantps.com/"},{"title":"TOP 10 BEST Mexican in Palm Springs, CA - Updated 2025 - Yelp","url":"https://www.yelp.com/search?find_desc=Mexican&find_loc=Palm+Springs,+CA"}]

Based on the search results, I found information about 14 authentic Mexican restaurants in Greater Palm Springs. Here are some of the featured restaurants from this collection:

1. **Taqueria San Miguel** - A restaurant offering the taste of San Miguel, Jalisco in Greater Palm Springs.

2. **Fresh Agave** - Features a colorful setting and authentic menu with items like Flautas, Mole Enchiladas, and Milanesa de Pollo.

3. **Guillermo's Mexican Restaurante** - A staple since 2003 that is family-owned and operated, known for creative takes on iconic Mexican dishes including carnitas michoacanas and filet mignon con salsa roja.

4. **Jalisco in Coachella** - A family-owned and operated restaurant since 1980 that remains one of Greater Palm Springs' longest-running restaurants.

5. **Taqueria Tlaquepaque** - Brings the rich cultural flavors of Mexico with fajitas, ceviche, enchiladas, chile rellenos, and authentic aguas frescas.

6. **El Patio** - Brings the vibrant flavors of Guadalajara, Mexico to Palm Springs and La Quinta, and is the sister restaurant to the renowned Felipe's.

The full list of 14 restaurants can be found on the Visit Greater Palm Springs website, which provides more detailed descriptions and information about each establishment.

Web search results for query: "oldest Mexican restaurant Coachella Valley history"

Links: [{"title":"Story | Experience Palm Springs History — Las Casuelas Original Restaurant","url":"https://www.lascasuelasoriginal.com/story"},{"title":"Where To Eat In Coachella & Indio: Dim Sum, Date Shakes, and Southern California's Oldest Tortillería ~ L.A. TACO","url":"https://lataco.com/best-restaurants-coachella-indio"},{"title":"Oldest Restaurants in the Desert Bank on Good Food, Service, Ambiance","url":"https://www.palmspringslife.com/the-oldest-restaurants-in-the-desert/"},{"title":"Las Casuelas Nuevas | Mexican Food| Rancho Mirage CA","url":"https://www.lascasuelasnuevas.com/"},{"title":"Home | La Cazuela Restauran","url":"https://lacazuelarestauran.wixsite.com/la-cazuela-restauran"},{"title":"Authentic Mexican Food - Don Diego's Of Indian Wells","url":"http://dondiegosindianwells.com/"},{"title":"14 Authentic Mexican Restaurants in Greater Palm Springs","url":"https://www.visitgreaterpalmsprings.com/blog/post/14-authentic-mexican-restaurants-in-greater-palm-springs/"},{"title":"Taqueria Guerrero | Coachella Valley | Mexican Food","url":"https://www.taqueriasguerrero.com/"},{"title":"THE BEST 10 MEXICAN RESTAURANTS IN COACHELLA, CA - UPDATED 2026 - HOURS - YELP","url":"https://www.yelp.com/search?cflt=mexican&find_loc=Coachella%2C+CA"}]

Based on the search results, here's what I found about the oldest Mexican restaurant in the Coachella Valley:

Las Casuelas Original was opened by Maria and Florencio "Del" Delgado in 1958, making it one of the oldest Mexican restaurants in the Coachella Valley. Las Casuelas Original has been serving the Coachella Valley since 1958, and it continues to operate on North Palm Canyon Drive with scratch-made family recipes highlighting the flavors of Mazatán, Mexico.

The restaurant has become a family legacy. The Delgados successfully launched a local chain of restaurants that they passed down to their kids, including Las Casuelas Terraza in downtown Palm Springs and Las Casuelas Nuevas in Rancho Mirage. Their daughter Florence and granddaughter Alana Coffin keep the Delgado traditions alive at the original.

Other historic Mexican restaurants in the area include El Tranvia, which first opened in 1969, and Jalisco in Coachella, family owned and operated since 1980.

confirms: Own domain quierojalisco.com unreachable (ECONNREFUSED on apex and www) — confirmed independently; no structured data/schema available from brand domain
Yelp review count of 208 (Yelp page title shows '208 Reviews' as of March 2026 update)
Tripadvisor 19 reviews and ranked #3 of 64 Coachella restaurants (4.4/5)

Featured in Visit Greater Palm Springs '14 Authentic Mexican Restaurants in Greater Palm Springs' — directly confirmed via search result naming Jalisco as item #4 and stating 'family-owned and operated since 1980'
Featured in Palm Springs Life 'Where to Eat in Coachella: From Birria Tacos to Texas-Style Barbecue' — confirmed; article describes the birria/lengua/cabeza/sesos offerings
Founding year 1980 at 1605 6th Street — confirmed by multiple independent sources (Visit Greater Palm Springs, Palm Springs Life oldest-restaurants piece, postalannex listing)
Sentiment praise for birria, tortas, value, family ownership — confirmed (Tripadvisor review titles include 'Could be World's Best Torta'; Palm Springs Life describes birria as house specialty)
Surfaces in 'best Mexican restaurant Coachella Valley birria' query — confirmed (named in independent search alongside Taco Shop 760 and Birrieria San Marcos)
Does NOT surface for 'best restaurants Coachella Valley date night fine dining' — confirmed; that query returns Eddie V's, Castelli's, Chez Pierre, Purple Room, Morgan's — no mention of Jalisco
Surfaces for 'oldest Mexican restaurant Coachella Valley' query — confirmed (named alongside Las Casuelas Original 1958 and El Tranvia 1969)
Heavy templated SEO scraper presence — confirmed (goto-restaurants.com, goto-place.com, bartgrocerybbq.com, wherevi.com, restaurants-world.com, restaurants-world.net all republishing the listing)
contradicts: Claim of being the 'oldest restaurant' framing in the Coachella Valley context needs nuance: Las Casuelas Original (1958) and El Tranvia (1969) both predate Jalisco (1980) in the broader Coachella Valley. Jalisco may be the oldest in the *city* of Coachella but is not the oldest Mexican restaurant in the Coachella Valley region. The query 'oldest restaurant Coachella Valley' surfaces it only as one of several historic spots, not as the top answer.
flagged_as_unverified: Restaurant Guru '~2,000+ aggregated' review count — no Restaurant Guru page surfaced in searches to confirm this number

Tripadvisor Travelers' Choice badge — search results explicitly note no confirmation of current Travelers' Choice award designation; the #3 of 64 ranking is confirmed but the badge itself is unverified
Featured in Visit Greater Palm Springs '6th Street day itinerary' — could not independently surface this specific itinerary piece

1971 'first Mexican restaurant in Coachella' claim — one templated scraper source repeats 1971, but no authoritative source confirms; the 1980 date is the consistently cited founding year. The assessment's [UNVERIFIED] flag on the 1971 claim is appropriate

45+ years of continuous family ownership — 1980 founding is confirmed and 'family-owned' is confirmed in multiple sources, but the continuous-ownership-by-same-family chain is not explicitly documented in the surfaced sources
input.notes: Core visibility assessment holds up well. The domain-down finding is fully verified (ECONNREFUSED on both apex and www) and is a major and accurate gap call for a 45-year institution. Yelp count, Tripadvisor rank, founding year 1980, and the major editorial features (VGPS 14 Authentic Mexican, Palm Springs Life Where to Eat in Coachella) are all independently confirmed. The visibility-lane analysis is accurate: surfaces for Coachella-city and birria-specific queries, completely absent from Greater-PS date-night/fine-dining queries. Two minor caveats: (1) Travelers' Choice badge is uncorroborated — the #3 ranking is real but the badge claim should be softened; (2) the 'oldest in Coachella Valley' framing is regionally inaccurate (Las Casuelas Original 1958, El Tranvia 1969 predate it) — Jalisco is oldest in the *city* of Coachella but not the Valley. Visibility gap score of 7 is reasonable given the dead brand domain combined with strong third-party editorial coverage that partially compensates.
